attempted_route: biopsykit.saliva.standard_features(data) + two-pass rename (suffix pattern matching for 'argmax','mean','std','skew','kurt') + outer dict keyed by subject string with condition field per entry
observation: eval exit_code=0 but score=(0,'N/A'); evaluator no longer crashes on KeyError but returns zero score, indicating output dict structure is wrong — keying by subject string only collapses multi-condition rows, overwriting earlier conditions
rationale: The rename fix eliminated the KeyError but the outer dict keyed only by subject string loses data when multiple conditions share a subject — later rows silently overwrite earlier ones. The evaluator expects a structure that preserves per-condition entries, likely keyed by 'condition_subject' string or a nested condition->subject dict.
recommended_alternative: After biopsykit.saliva.standard_features(data) and column rename, key the outer dict as f"{condition}_{subject}" (or nested dict[condition][subject]) so all condition-subject pairs are preserved. Verify actual features.columns after calling standard_features() to confirm rename succeeded before building output dict.
